sanskrit: सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्याः प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि । प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रहः किं करिष्यति ॥
hindiTranslation: सम्पूर्ण प्राणी प्रकृतिको प्राप्त होते हैं ।  ज्ञानी महापुरुष भी अपनी प्रकृतिके अनुसार चेष्टा करता है ।  फिर इसमें किसीका हठ क्या करेगा ॥
englishTranslation: Even the wise act according to their own nature. All beings follow nature; what can restraint do?
essence: Nature drives behavior; force alone cannot overrule it.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Even a wise person acts according to their own nature. Every living being follows its nature, so force alone cannot change that.
simpleInsight: There is relief in seeing that not everything yields to willpower.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna explains that deep tendencies shape behavior: even the wise act from their nature. This verse prepares Arjuna to understand why mere force cannot solve inner conflict.
modernRelevance: You promise yourself you will not check the phone again, then your hand reaches for it before you notice. Krishna points to the harder truth: habits run deep, and brute force alone rarely wins.
translationLiteral: सदृशं (according to) / चेष्टते (acts) / स्वस्याः (of one's own) / प्रकृतेः (nature) / ज्ञानवान् अपि (even the wise) / प्रकृतिम् (nature) / यान्ति (go to) / भूतानि (all beings) / निग्रहः (restraint) / किम् (what) / करिष्यति (will do)
insight: It shifts attention from moral self-blame to honest observation of ingrained tendencies.

quoteVariants.text: Even the wise move by nature. Restraint cannot rewrite what beings are.
Nature moves all. Restraint cannot remake it.
I can force behavior, but not erase nature.
See nature clearly. Stop fighting its pull.
